enrichment.short_description: Authentication and user management platform built specifically for B2B SaaS products, with organizations, roles, and enterprise SSO as first-class concepts.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription based on monthly active users (MAUs), with a free tier, paid Growth and Growth Plus plans, and custom-quoted Enterprise pricing for larger deployments.
enrichment.starting_price: Free for up to 10,500 monthly active users; Growth plan starts at $150 per month
enrichment.seo_title: PropelAuth Review 2026: Features, Pricing and B2B Auth Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how PropelAuth's B2B-focused authentication platform works in 2026, including pricing, SSO and SCIM support, key features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: PropelAuth is an authentication and user management platform founded in 2021 and headquartered in Redwood City, California, built by a founding team with prior experience building authentication systems for both enterprise and startup clients across B2B and B2C markets. The company went through Y Combinator's Winter 2022 batch and has raised a total of roughly 3.09 million dollars, including a 2.59 million dollar seed round, from investors including Y Combinator, Tiger Global, 8-Bit Capital, Soma Capital, Flex Capital, Crosstimbers Capital Group, Forward Deployed VC, and individual angel investors. PropelAuth's core positioning is that it is purpose-built for B2B SaaS authentication specifically, rather than being a general-purpose identity platform adapted for B2B use cases after the fact. The defining architectural choice is that organizations are a first-class concept throughout the product: rather than authenticating individual users in isolation, PropelAuth models the reality that most B2B customers are companies with many employees who need different roles and permissions, and a single user account can belong to multiple organizations with different roles in each. Every authenticated request carries the user's organization membership, roles, and permissions, and organizations can be configured to let new users auto-join via a verified company email domain, removing manual invite friction for larger customer accounts. Enterprise-readiness features are deeply integrated into this same organization model rather than bolted on separately: SAML and OIDC single sign-on and SCIM directory provisioning are supported out of the box for identity providers including Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud, and PropelAuth is explicit that enabling SSO for a customer requires no code changes, since customers can self-configure their own SSO connection through guided setup flows. Additional features include configurable password policies, enforced multi-factor authentication, per-organization session controls, user impersonation for support and debugging (built with safety guardrails and audit alerting), webhook events for user and organization lifecycle actions, a Terraform provider for managing auth configuration as code, and the ability for users and organizations to generate their own API keys that automatically invalidate if the associated user or organization is blocked or deleted. PropelAuth's pricing is structured around monthly active users: a free tier covers up to 10,500 MAUs with unlimited organizations, a Growth plan at 150 dollars per month adds user impersonation, unlimited enterprise SSO connections at no extra per-connection fee, a separate staging environment, and API key management, with additional users beyond the plan's included volume billed incrementally with volume discounts; a Growth Plus plan at 500 dollars per month adds SCIM directory sync and more advanced organization and security controls; and Enterprise customers get custom-quoted pricing and terms. A notable competitive point PropelAuth emphasizes is that all paid plans include unlimited SSO connections with no per-connection fees, which is a meaningful differentiator against some competing identity platforms that charge incrementally per enterprise SSO connection as a customer's B2B customer base grows. As a small, venture-backed team (estimated at fewer than ten employees as of 2026), PropelAuth is best suited to B2B SaaS companies, particularly startups and mid-market software companies, that need enterprise-ready authentication (SSO, SCIM, organizations, RBAC) without building and maintaining it themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: PropelAuth is a B2B-focused authentication and user management platform founded in 2021 and headquartered in Redwood City, California. The company went through Y Combinator's Winter 2022 batch and has raised roughly 3.09 million dollars from investors including Y Combinator, Tiger Global, and Soma Capital.

Unlike general-purpose identity platforms adapted for B2B use cases, PropelAuth is built from the ground up around the reality of B2B SaaS: customers are companies with many employees, different roles, and enterprise IT requirements like SSO and SCIM, not just individual end users.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Organizations are a first-class concept throughout PropelAuth: users can belong to multiple organizations with different roles in each, organizations can auto-join users via verified company email domains, and every authenticated request carries the user's organization membership, roles, and permissions.

Enterprise-readiness features, including SAML and OIDC SSO and SCIM directory provisioning for providers like Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud, are deeply integrated rather than bolted on, and customers can self-configure their own SSO connections without requiring code changes from the PropelAuth customer.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: PropelAuth's pricing is based on monthly active users (MAUs). The free tier covers up to 10,500 MAUs with unlimited organizations, while the Growth plan at 150 dollars per month adds unlimited enterprise SSO connections, user impersonation, a staging environment, and API key management.

A Growth Plus plan at 500 dollars per month adds SCIM directory sync and advanced organization and security controls, while Enterprise customers receive custom pricing. Notably, all paid plans include unlimited SSO connections with no per-connection fees.

features.title: Organizations as a first-class concept
features.description: Model B2B customers as organizations with multiple users, each holding different roles per organization they belong to.

features.title: SAML and OIDC single sign-on
features.description: Support enterprise SSO for providers like Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud with no code changes required.

features.title: SCIM directory provisioning
features.description: Sync user provisioning and deprovisioning automatically with customers' identity providers via SCIM.

features.title: Self-serve SSO setup
features.description: Let your B2B customers configure their own SSO connections through guided flows without requiring engineering support.

features.title: User impersonation for support
features.description: Safely impersonate a user for debugging or support purposes, with built-in guardrails and audit alerting.

features.title: Configurable security policies
features.description: Enforce password policies, multi-factor authentication, and per-organization session controls.

features.title: Webhooks and Terraform provider
features.description: React to user and organization lifecycle events via webhooks, and manage auth configuration as code with a dedicated Terraform provider.

features.title: Self-service API keys
features.description: Let users and organizations generate their own API keys, which automatically invalidate if the associated account is blocked or deleted.

pros: Purpose-built for B2B SaaS with organizations, roles, and permissions modeled as core concepts rather than add-ons
Unlimited SSO connections included on paid plans with no per-connection fees, unlike some competitors
Generous free tier covering up to 10,500 monthly active users
Self-serve SSO and SCIM setup reduces engineering overhead when onboarding enterprise customers
cons: Small team size (estimated under 10 employees) means less scale than larger, more established identity providers
Less brand recognition than major identity platforms like Auth0 or Okta, which may matter for enterprise procurement checklists
SCIM directory sync requires stepping up to the pricier Growth Plus plan
As a young, venture-backed startup, long-term product roadmap and company stability carry more uncertainty than incumbents

faqs.question: What is PropelAuth used for
faqs.answer: PropelAuth is used by B2B SaaS companies to add authentication, organization management, and enterprise features like SSO and SCIM to their products without building it themselves.

faqs.question: How much does PropelAuth cost
faqs.answer: PropelAuth is free for up to 10,500 monthly active users. The Growth plan starts at 150 dollars per month, Growth Plus is 500 dollars per month, and Enterprise pricing is custom quoted.

faqs.question: Does PropelAuth charge extra per SSO connection
faqs.answer: No, all of PropelAuth's paid plans include unlimited enterprise SSO connections with no additional per-connection fees.

faqs.question: Does PropelAuth support SCIM provisioning
faqs.answer: Yes, SCIM directory sync is available starting on the Growth Plus plan, supporting providers like Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, OneLogin, and JumpCloud.

faqs.question: Who founded PropelAuth
faqs.answer: PropelAuth was founded in 2021 and went through Y Combinator's Winter 2022 batch, raising roughly 3.09 million dollars from investors including Y Combinator, Tiger Global, and Soma Capital.

faqs.question: Can a user belong to multiple organizations in PropelAuth
faqs.answer: Yes, PropelAuth models organizations as a first-class concept, and a single user can belong to multiple organizations with different roles in each.

faqs.question: Where is PropelAuth headquartered
faqs.answer: PropelAuth is headquartered in Redwood City, California, United States.

faqs.question: Does enabling SSO for a customer require code changes
faqs.answer: No, PropelAuth is designed so that customers can self-configure their own SSO connections through guided setup flows without requiring code changes.

use_cases.title: Enterprise-ready B2B SaaS onboarding
use_cases.description: SaaS companies use PropelAuth to quickly offer SSO and SCIM to enterprise customers during procurement without building identity infrastructure in-house.
use_cases.audience: B2B SaaS startups and mid-market software companies selling to enterprise buyers

use_cases.title: Multi-tenant organization management
use_cases.description: Products with complex customer structures use PropelAuth's organization model to manage users who belong to multiple companies with different roles in each.
use_cases.audience: B2B platforms serving customers with multiple departments, teams, or partner organizations

use_cases.title: Reducing engineering overhead on auth
use_cases.description: Engineering teams offload authentication, session management, and enterprise identity features to PropelAuth instead of maintaining custom auth infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams at growing startups that want to focus on core product rather than auth infrastructure

enrichment.short_description: Real estate CRM platform built on Salesforce for brokerages and agents, now operated as Propertybase powered by Lone Wolf Technologies.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-user monthly subscription with a minimum seat commitment for the Salesforce Edition; the separate Propertybase GO product uses custom, quote-based pricing.
enrichment.starting_price: From $79 per user per month (Salesforce Edition, 10-user minimum, roughly $790/month minimum)
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 500-750 employees (Lone Wolf Technologies, the parent company)
enrichment.headquarters: Cambridge, Ontario, Canada (Lone Wolf Technologies headquarters); Propertybase originated in West Boxford, Massachusetts, United States and Munich, Germany
enrichment.seo_title: Propertybase Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Lone Wolf Ownership Explained
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Propertybase's Salesforce-based real estate CRM works in 2026 under Lone Wolf Technologies ownership, including pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Propertybase is a real estate customer relationship management platform founded in 2010 that has gone through multiple rounds of acquisition on its way to becoming part of Lone Wolf Technologies, one of the largest real estate technology companies in North America. Munich-based Propertybase GmbH was originally acquired by US firm Boston Logic in June 2017, and then in August 2021 Lone Wolf Technologies, a Vista Equity Partners portfolio company, acquired Propertybase outright, describing the deal as completing real estate's technology platform for agents and brokers by combining Propertybase's CRM and marketing capabilities with Lone Wolf's existing transaction management and back-office software. Today the product is marketed as Propertybase powered by Lone Wolf and comes in two distinct editions: Propertybase GO, a CRM and website solution aimed at brokerages and teams with simplified setup and quote-based pricing, and Propertybase Salesforce Edition, an enterprise-class CRM built natively on the Salesforce platform for larger brokerages that want deep customization and Salesforce's full reporting and workflow ecosystem. The Salesforce Edition requires a minimum of 10 user licenses at 79 dollars per user per month, which includes the underlying Force.com platform license, putting the practical minimum entry cost at around 790 dollars per month, billed monthly, quarterly, or annually depending on the contract. Core capabilities span the real estate transaction lifecycle: IDX and MLS-integrated websites with real-time listing synchronization for lead capture, automated lead routing and matching using round robin, auto-assign, or click-to-claim rules, integrated email marketing with segmentation and drip automation for nurture sequences and transaction-milestone communications, transaction management integration tying into Lone Wolf's broader back-office and transaction software, and reporting and analytics that range from pipeline and agent performance reports up to full custom Salesforce dashboards on the Salesforce Edition. Mobile apps for iOS and Android provide contact management, activity logging, calendar sync, and deal pipeline visibility for agents working in the field. Lone Wolf Technologies, the parent company, is headquartered in Cambridge, Ontario, Canada, with a significant presence in Dallas, Texas, and reported employee counts across sources ranging from roughly 500 to nearly 750 people as of 2025, reflecting a company that has grown substantially through acquisitions across more than 30 years in the real estate software industry under Vista Equity Partners ownership. Given its Salesforce-native architecture, per-seat pricing, and minimum user requirements, Propertybase Salesforce Edition is positioned primarily toward mid-size and larger real estate brokerages that already value or use the Salesforce ecosystem and need deep CRM customization, while smaller teams and independent brokerages are more likely to be steered toward the simplified Propertybase GO product or Lone Wolf's other, less complex offerings.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Propertybase is a real estate CRM platform originally founded in 2010 that changed ownership twice: acquired by Boston Logic in 2017, and then acquired outright by Lone Wolf Technologies, a Vista Equity Partners portfolio company, in August 2021. It is now marketed as Propertybase powered by Lone Wolf.

The product line includes two distinct offerings: Propertybase GO, a simplified CRM and website solution for brokerages and teams, and Propertybase Salesforce Edition, an enterprise-class CRM built natively on the Salesforce platform for larger, more customization-hungry organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Propertybase centers on IDX and MLS-integrated websites with real-time listing sync for lead capture, paired with automated lead routing and matching rules such as round robin, auto-assign, and click-to-claim to get new leads to the right agent quickly.

Beyond lead generation, the platform includes integrated email marketing with segmentation and drip automation, transaction management integration with Lone Wolf's back-office systems, pipeline and agent-performance reporting, and native mobile apps for iOS and Android that give agents contact management and deal pipeline visibility on the go.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Propertybase Salesforce Edition requires a minimum of 10 user licenses at 79 dollars per user per month, which includes the underlying Force.com platform license, putting the effective minimum monthly cost at around 790 dollars for a brokerage's first 10 seats.

Propertybase GO, the simplified CRM and website product, is priced separately on a custom, quote basis rather than published per-seat pricing, and is generally positioned as the more accessible entry point for smaller brokerages and teams.

features.title: IDX and MLS-integrated websites
features.description: Lead-capture websites with real-time MLS listing synchronization built for real estate search and browsing.

features.title: Automated lead routing
features.description: Route and assign new leads to agents automatically using round robin, auto-assign, or click-to-claim rules.

features.title: Integrated email marketing automation
features.description: Build segmented drip campaigns for nurture sequences, transaction-milestone updates, and broadcast communications.

features.title: Transaction management integration
features.description: Connects to Lone Wolf's broader transaction management and back-office software for end-to-end deal workflow.

features.title: Pipeline and performance reporting
features.description: Track pipeline status, agent performance, and campaign attribution, with full custom Salesforce dashboards on the Salesforce Edition.

features.title: Native Salesforce customization
features.description: The Salesforce Edition is built directly on the Salesforce platform, giving access to Salesforce's full customization, workflow, and reporting ecosystem.

features.title: Mobile apps for agents
features.description: iOS and Android apps provide contact management, activity logging, calendar sync, and pipeline access for agents in the field.

pros: Deep native Salesforce integration on the Salesforce Edition appeals to brokerages already invested in the Salesforce ecosystem
Backed by Lone Wolf Technologies' broader real estate software suite, including transaction management and back-office tools
Strong IDX and MLS integration with real-time listing synchronization for lead generation
Established product with roots going back to 2010 and continued investment under Lone Wolf and Vista Equity Partners ownership
cons: Salesforce Edition requires a 10-user minimum at 79 dollars per user per month, which can be a significant upfront cost for small teams
Complexity and Salesforce dependency mean a steeper learning curve and potentially higher implementation and administration costs
Two distinct product lines (GO and Salesforce Edition) can create confusion about which version fits a given brokerage's size and needs
Ownership changes across three companies since 2010 (original Propertybase, Boston Logic, and Lone Wolf) mean product direction has shifted more than once

faqs.question: Who owns Propertybase now
faqs.answer: Propertybase is owned by Lone Wolf Technologies, a Vista Equity Partners portfolio company, which acquired Propertybase in August 2021. The product is now marketed as Propertybase powered by Lone Wolf.

faqs.question: How much does Propertybase cost
faqs.answer: Propertybase Salesforce Edition starts at 79 dollars per user per month with a 10-user minimum, putting the minimum monthly cost around 790 dollars. Propertybase GO uses separate, custom quote-based pricing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Propertybase GO and Propertybase Salesforce Edition
faqs.answer: Propertybase GO is a simplified CRM and website solution for brokerages and teams, while Propertybase Salesforce Edition is a more customizable, enterprise-class CRM built natively on the Salesforce platform.

faqs.question: When was Propertybase founded
faqs.answer: Propertybase was founded in 2010 and has since been acquired twice, first by Boston Logic in 2017 and then by Lone Wolf Technologies in 2021.

faqs.question: Does Propertybase integrate with MLS listings
faqs.answer: Yes, Propertybase includes IDX-enabled, MLS-integrated websites with real-time listing synchronization for lead capture.

faqs.question: Is Propertybase good for small real estate teams
faqs.answer: The Salesforce Edition's 10-user minimum and per-seat pricing generally make it a better fit for mid-size and larger brokerages, while smaller teams are more often directed to the simplified Propertybase GO product.

faqs.question: Where is Propertybase's parent company headquartered
faqs.answer: Lone Wolf Technologies, Propertybase's parent company, is headquartered in Cambridge, Ontario, Canada, with a significant office presence in Dallas, Texas.

faqs.question: Does Propertybase have mobile apps
faqs.answer: Yes, Propertybase offers iOS and Android apps that give agents contact management, activity logging, calendar sync, and pipeline access on mobile.

use_cases.title: Enterprise brokerage CRM on Salesforce
use_cases.description: Larger brokerages already invested in the Salesforce ecosystem use Propertybase Salesforce Edition for deep CRM customization, reporting, and workflow automation.
use_cases.audience: Mid-size to large real estate brokerages using or wanting Salesforce-native infrastructure

use_cases.title: Lead generation and routing at scale
use_cases.description: Brokerages use Propertybase's IDX-integrated websites and automated lead routing to capture and distribute leads to agents efficiently.
use_cases.audience: Brokerages and teams focused on high-volume online lead generation

use_cases.title: End-to-end transaction workflow
use_cases.description: Organizations already using Lone Wolf's transaction management and back-office tools extend that workflow into CRM and marketing with Propertybase.
use_cases.audience: Brokerages standardized on the broader Lone Wolf real estate technology suite
